reasoning_model_1: The user command explicitly requested the model to say the sentence '啊！那里有只老鼠！' (Ah! There is a mouse there!) with a scream. However, Model 1 completely failed to adhere to the content constraints. Instead of performing the dialogue, it generated and read a long narrative description of a scene ('...screaming and jumping up, potato chips fell on the floor...'). Due to this severe hallucination and failure to follow the specific text script, the content accuracy is rated as 0. Consequently, it failed to produce the required 'non-verbal vocalization' (scream) as a paralinguistic feature acting on the target text; it merely read the word 'screaming' as part of a story. According to the scoring standard, extremely poor content results in a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 strictly followed the user's command. The content '啊！！！那里有只老鼠' matches the target script perfectly. The text suggests a high-intensity delivery ('!!!'). Assuming the audio corresponds to the text representation provided, the model successfully integrated the non-verbal vocalization (the scream 'Ah!!!') before the sentence, effectively conveying the requested paralinguistic feature of screaming/fear. The expression appears natural and aligned with the context of seeing a mouse.
model_comparison: Model 1 suffered from a critical failure in instruction following, generating a long, hallucinated narrative instead of the requested short dialogue. It failed to produce the target audio content entirely. Model 2, in contrast, followed the instructions accurately, delivering the correct text with the appropriate paralinguistic feature (scream). Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
